vBRIEFInfo.author: overdeck/0.0.0
vBRIEFInfo.description: Plan for PAN-2224: feat(sessions): multi-harness session discovery, search, enrichment + transcript rendering (pi/ohmypi/codex)

plan.title: feat(sessions): multi-harness session discovery, search, enrichment + transcript rendering (pi/ohmypi/codex)
plan.author: agent:claude-fable-5

references.label: PRD (glossary, verified refs, decisions D-1..D-11, work items WI-1..WI-8)

narratives.Problem: The Sessions page's discovery/search/enrichment pipeline is Claude Code only: the scanner walks only ~/.claude/projects/, parsing assumes Claude JSONL, FTS/enrichment/embeddings inherit the gap, archived pi/codex/ohmypi rows expose a fabricated Claude jsonlPath, and transcript resolution for managed pi/codex conversations dies after agent-dir cleanup because there is no discovered-sessions fallback. All 6,861 discovered_sessions rows are Claude Code while archived conversations span four harnesses.
narratives.Proposal: Follow the PRD at .pan/drafts/pan-2224.md exactly. Add a harness column to discovered_sessions (v57→v58 migration, backfill 'claude-code'); generalize discovery into per-harness sources in a new src/lib/conversations/harness-discovery.ts (claude projects dir, ~/.pi/agent/sessions, ~/.omp/agent/sessions, ~/.codex/sessions, per-agent dirs); add pi-v3 and codex nested-payload metadata parsers in a new src/lib/conversations/harness-metadata.ts returning the existing SessionMetadata shape; correlate non-Claude files to managed conversations by conversation_files.locator instead of Claude path reconstruction; make enrichment excerpts harness-dispatched so FTS covers all harnesses (embeddings need no change); fix the archived jsonlPath Claude-only fallback and add a discovered-sessions fallback to resolveSessionFile; surface harness as a facet + badge on today's Sessions surface (sessions-feed.ts has NOT landed). Transcript RENDERING already works for all harnesses via the harness-dispatched messages endpoint — only resolution/fallback is in scope.
narratives.NonGoals:
- Sessions page layout/UX redesign (PAN-1917; only the harness facet/badge touches UI here)
- New transcript renderers (rendering already works via the harness-dispatched messages endpoint; only resolution/fallback changes)
- Devcontainer/remote (Fly) session roots — host-local dirs only
- Changes to the frozen legacy data-layer twins (PAN-1983/PAN-1984)
- Cost-event ledger backfill for ad-hoc sessions (discovery records token-estimated cost only)
- New CLI verbs (existing scan trigger covers all harnesses, per D-10)

autoDecisions.summary: D-11 resolved: PAN-1917's unified feed (src/lib/overdeck/sessions-feed.ts) has NOT landed in this workspace, so the harness facet/badge bead targets today's surface: DiscoveredSessionSnapshot contracts, buildFilterSql, routes/discovered-sessions.ts params, FacetPanel.tsx, SessionTable.tsx (both under src/dashboard/frontend/src/components/conversations/).
autoDecisions.rationale: Verified by file absence in this worktree at planning time; the PRD's D-11 prescribed exactly this branch when the feed is absent.

autoDecisions.summary: D-6 codex-cwd implementation checkpoint resolved during planning: a real rollout under ~/.codex/sessions/ begins with {"type":"session_meta","payload":{"id":"<uuid>","cwd":"/abs/path",...}} — workspacePath comes from payload.cwd of the session_meta line, session id from payload.id, thread id from the filename suffix. The agent-dir/agents-table fallback remains for rollouts missing session_meta.
autoDecisions.rationale: Inspected /home/eltmon/.codex/sessions/2026/06/17/rollout-2026-06-17T03-44-34-019ed48a-....jsonl directly; removing the checkpoint eliminates a judgment call from the work role.

autoDecisions.summary: Deliver as one issue (one vBRIEF), decomposed into 11 beads: baseline measurement, harness column, discovery sources, pi parser, codex parser, locator correlation, enrichment excerpts, jsonlPath guard, transcript fallback, harness facet/badge UI, and the closing scan re-measurement.
autoDecisions.rationale: The issue body mandates delivery as one complete feature per repo policy; the PRD's WI-3 splits cleanly into two independently testable parser beads and WI-6/WI-8 into independent halves, maximizing reviewability without partial delivery.

autoDecisions.summary: requiresInspection limited to the two foundation beads: the schema/contracts column (cross-cutting schema) and the discovery-source architecture (interface every later bead consumes). All parser/UI/fix beads surface defects immediately via fixture tests or typecheck.
autoDecisions.rationale: Matches the inspection rubric: only these two beads' internal shape is load-bearing for downstream beads; the rest are leaves or mechanically verifiable.

autoDecisions.summary: Scan-timing measurements are recorded both as a comment on PAN-2224 and appended to .pan/drafts/pan-2224.md under a '## Scan timing' heading.
autoDecisions.rationale: The issue AC requires before/after measurement; recording in the PRD draft keeps the evidence in-repo where review can verify it.

items.title: Measure baseline system-scan time on the live Claude-only corpus

expected_outputs: PRD draft contains a '## Scan timing' section with a dated baseline duration for a system scan of the ~6.9k-file corpus
metadata.acJustification: Measurement-only bead; two criteria fully cover it.

narrative.Action: BEFORE any scanner changes land, time a full `system` scan of the live ~6.9k-session Claude corpus. Trigger it via the existing scan surface (POST /api/discovered-sessions/scan with mode 'system' against the local dashboard, or the scanConversations RPC — see src/dashboard/server/routes/discovered-sessions.ts). Record the wall-clock duration (run twice, report both) in two places: append a '## Scan timing' section with a 'Baseline (pre-PAN-2224)' entry to .pan/drafts/pan-2224.md, and post the same numbers as a comment on GitHub issue 2224 (gh issue comment 2224). This bead changes no source code.

items.title: Given the unmodified scanner, when a system scan runs on the live corpus, the measured duration is recorded under '## Scan timing' in .pan/drafts/pan-2224.md with a date and run count
A comment on GitHub issue 2224 contains the same baseline numbers
Add harness column to discovered_sessions: schema v58, data layer, contracts, snapshot/REST

expected_outputs: typecheck completes without errors
schema test file passes including new harness-column and backfill assertions

narrative.Action: Implement PRD WI-1 exactly. (1) In initDiscoveredSessionsSchema (src/lib/database/schema.ts:66) add `harness TEXT` to the CREATE TABLE. (2) Bump SCHEMA_VERSION 57→58 (schema.ts:24) and add to runMigrations (near schema.ts:739) the idempotent migration: `if (currentVersion < 58) { try { db.exec('ALTER TABLE discovered_sessions ADD COLUMN harness TEXT'); } catch { /* already exists */ } db.exec("UPDATE discovered_sessions SET harness = 'claude-code' WHERE harness IS NULL"); }` — copy the try/catch ALTER pattern from the v33 migration (schema.ts:1216-1218). (3) Add `harness: string` to UpsertDiscoveredSessionOpts (src/lib/overdeck/discovered-sessions.ts:382-403) and thread it through the upsert SQL and rowToSession. Existing scanner call sites pass 'claude-code' for now (per-harness values arrive in later beads). (4) Contracts: add optional `harness` to DiscoveredSessionSnapshot (packages/contracts/src/types.ts:628-659); map it in toDiscoveredSessionSnapshot (src/dashboard/server/ws-rpc.ts:418) and the REST response schema (src/dashboard/server/routes/discovered-sessions.ts:116-146). Rebuild contracts after editing (cd packages/contracts && npm run build). Do NOT touch the frozen legacy data-layer twins.

items.title: Given a DB at schema v57, when migrations run, discovered_sessions gains a harness column and every pre-existing row reads harness='claude-code'
Given an upsert with harness set, when the row is read back through the data layer, rowToSession returns the same harness value (round-trip test)
Given a row with harness set, DiscoveredSessionSnapshot and the REST list response return the harness field
Given an already-migrated DB, when migrations re-run, the idempotent ALTER exits without error
Per-harness discovery sources module + scanner integration

expected_outputs: typecheck completes without errors
scanner tests pass including new per-harness enumeration fixtures and the unchanged-Claude-enumeration regression guard
metadata.readinessReason: Deliberately serialized: blocked on the harness-column schema bead and the scan-baseline measurement; restructures scanner.ts that the parser and correlation beads then modify.

narrative.Action: Implement PRD WI-2 exactly, per D-1/D-3. Create src/lib/conversations/harness-discovery.ts exporting `DiscoverySource = { harness, roots: () => string[], collect: (root) => Promise<DiscoveredFile[]> }` and `DiscoveredFile = { jsonlPath, projectDir, harness }`. Sources: claude-code (extract the existing ~/.claude/projects walk from discoverJsonlFiles, scanner.ts:107-134, verbatim); pi (~/.pi/agent/sessions); ohmypi (~/.omp/agent/sessions — same encoded-cwd/<timestamp>_<session-id>.jsonl layout); codex (~/.codex/sessions, recursive YYYY/MM/DD walk — mirror walkForThread in src/lib/runtimes/codex.ts:109-132); agent-dirs (iterate ~/.overdeck/agents/*/: `sessions/` → pi-family tagged per D-1 (agent's recorded harness where knowable, else ohmypi), agent-dir-root `*_*.jsonl` pi work-agent files (PAN-1908), `codex-home/sessions/` → codex). Missing roots are skipped silently. discoverJsonlFiles concatenates all sources; targeted/watched modes filter after resolution exactly as today; ScanOptions keeps its external shape. All FS access via fs/promises — zero sync FS calls (scanner.ts:12 doctrine). Files from non-Claude sources are tagged but may be skipped by scan() until the parser beads land — gate metadata parsing on the harness tag so this bead is independently mergeable with Claude behavior byte-identical.

items.title: Given tmp-dir fixture trees for all four harness roots plus agent dirs, when discovery runs, every fixture file is enumerated with the correct harness tag (claude-code/pi/ohmypi/codex per D-1)
Given only Claude fixtures, when discovery runs, enumeration output is identical to the pre-change scanner (regression guard test)
Given a machine missing some harness roots, when discovery runs, missing roots are skipped silently and the scan completes without warnings for them
harness-discovery.ts contains no synchronous fs calls (fs/promises only) and scanner.ts orchestration keeps its ScanOptions shape
Pi v3 metadata parser (pi + ohmypi) wired into scan dispatch

expected_outputs: typecheck completes without errors
harness-metadata tests pass: pi fixture yields correct SessionMetadata on every field plus a malformed-line tolerance case

narrative.Action: Implement the pi half of PRD WI-3, per D-4. Create src/lib/conversations/harness-metadata.ts exporting `parsePiSessionMetadata(path): Promise<SessionMetadata>` (SessionMetadata shape: src/lib/conversations/jsonl-async.ts:28-42) using a streaming line reader (pattern: jsonl-async.ts:104). Session id + cwd from the first line `{ type: 'session', version: 3, id, timestamp, cwd }`; message count from `type:'message'` lines; first/last timestamps from line timestamps; per-call usage summed from input/output/cacheRead/cacheWrite (field logic: src/lib/cost-parsers/pi-parser.ts:38-51); tools from toolCall blocks inside message content; models from model_change events (provider/modelId lines — verified in a real ~/.pi session). estimatedCost: reuse the exact mechanism scanner.ts:298-318 uses for Claude (tokens + getModelCapabilitySync). In scan() (scanner.ts:274, `const meta = await parseJsonl(jsonlPath);`), dispatch on the DiscoveredFile harness tag: pi/ohmypi → parsePiSessionMetadata; claude-code path untouched. Upsert passes the file's harness. Fixtures: a Pi v3 session including a toolCall and a model_change; a malformed-line case that is tolerated (skipped, not thrown).

items.title: Given a Pi v3 fixture with a toolCall and model_change, when parsed, SessionMetadata returns correct sessionId, cwd, messageCount, first/last timestamps, models, token in/out, tools, and estimated cost
Given a fixture containing a malformed JSONL line, when parsed, the parser skips it and still returns metadata for the valid lines
Given a scan over a pi/ohmypi fixture tree, when it completes, discovered_sessions rows persist with harness set and the parsed metadata populated
Codex rollout metadata parser (nested-payload schema) wired into scan dispatch

expected_outputs: typecheck completes without errors
harness-metadata tests pass: codex rollout fixture yields correct SessionMetadata including token_count usage and function_call tools

narrative.Action: Implement the codex half of PRD WI-3, per D-4 and the resolved D-6. Add `parseCodexSessionMetadata(path): Promise<SessionMetadata>` to src/lib/conversations/harness-metadata.ts using the NESTED-PAYLOAD rollout schema (parsed today by src/dashboard/server/services/codex-conversation-parser.ts:123-152) — NEVER extractCodexTranscript (src/lib/session-format-converter.ts:138), which is a flat seed-file schema only. First line is `{ type: 'session_meta', payload: { id, timestamp, cwd, ... } }` (verified on a real rollout): session id = payload.id (cross-check thread id from the filename suffix `-<threadId>.jsonl`, matching src/lib/runtimes/codex.ts:127); workspacePath = payload.cwd. Message count from event_msg payload.type user_message/agent_message; usage from token_count events' total_token_usage (field logic: src/lib/cost-parsers/codex-parser.ts:25-38, tolerating flat vs nested CLI variants); tools from response_item function_call names. estimatedCost: same scanner mechanism as Claude (scanner.ts:298-318). Wire the codex branch into the scan() harness dispatch. Fixtures: a rollout including session_meta, user_message, agent_message, token_count, and a function_call; a malformed-line tolerance case.

items.title: Given a nested-payload rollout fixture, when parsed, SessionMetadata returns sessionId from session_meta payload.id, cwd from payload.cwd, correct message count, usage from total_token_usage, and function_call tool names
Given a fixture with a malformed line, when parsed, the parser skips it and returns metadata from the valid lines
Given a scan over a codex sessions fixture tree (YYYY/MM/DD layout), when it completes, discovered_sessions rows persist with harness='codex' and parsed metadata populated
Locator-based managed-conversation correlation + non-Claude workspace resolution

expected_outputs: typecheck completes without errors
correlator + scanner tests pass including managed-ohmypi correlation and codex workspace-fallback cases

narrative.Action: Implement PRD WI-4, per D-5/D-6. In buildCorrelationMapSync (src/lib/conversations/correlator.ts:27): alongside the existing Claude candidate-path branch (correlator.ts:51 — leave untouched), build a Map<locator, {name, issueId}> from the same query's conversation_files rows and match non-Claude files by their parsed sessionId, setting overdeckManaged and panIssueId. In the scanner resolution step (scanner.ts:277): for non-Claude files skip HashResolver (it is Claude-path-shaped); workspacePath = the pi first-line cwd for pi-family, payload.cwd from session_meta for codex (already parsed in the metadata beads); if a codex rollout lacks session_meta, resolve via the owning agent dir → agents-table workspace, else leave workspacePath null (column is nullable). Create src/lib/conversations/__tests__/correlator.test.ts (none exists today). Tests: a managed ohmypi fixture correlates (overdeckManaged=1, panIssueId set); an ad-hoc pi fixture stays unmanaged; the codex agent-dir workspace fallback is covered.

items.title: Given a managed ohmypi session whose locator exists in conversation_files, when scanned, its discovered row persists overdeckManaged=1 and the correct panIssueId without any Claude path reconstruction
Given an ad-hoc pi session with no matching locator, when scanned, the row persists as unmanaged
Given a codex rollout without session_meta owned by a known agent dir, when scanned, workspacePath resolves via the agents table; with no owner it defaults to null
The existing Claude correlation branch produces unchanged results for Claude fixtures (branch untouched)
Harness-dispatched enrichment excerpt builders (pi + codex)

expected_outputs: typecheck completes without errors
enrichment tests pass including non-empty pi/codex excerpts and the byte-identical Claude excerpt regression guard

narrative.Action: Implement PRD WI-5, per D-7. In src/lib/conversations/enrichment/enrich-session.ts, make buildConversationExcerpt (line ~186) dispatch on the session row's harness (available since the harness-column bead): buildPiExcerpt (role/content from the `message` object of type:'message' lines; summarize toolCall names + truncated args; include toolResult snippets) and buildCodexExcerpt (user_message/agent_message payload text; function_call names from response_item lines) — both returning the same excerpt-string shape the enrichment prompt already consumes. NO FTS changes: enriched pi/codex rows enter sessions_fts via the existing replaceFtsRow path (src/lib/overdeck/discovered-sessions.ts:422-436). NO embedding changes: buildEmbeddingText embeds enriched metadata only (src/lib/conversations/embeddings/index.ts:90-100). Tests: pi and codex fixture lines produce non-empty excerpts containing the user text and tool names; the Claude excerpt output is byte-identical to before (regression guard).

items.title: Given pi fixture lines, when the excerpt builder runs for a harness='ohmypi' row, it produces a non-empty excerpt containing the user message text and toolCall names
Given codex rollout fixture lines, when the excerpt builder runs for a harness='codex' row, it produces a non-empty excerpt containing user/agent message text and function_call names
Given Claude fixture lines, the excerpt output is byte-identical to the pre-change builder (regression test)
Given an enriched pi or codex row, when FTS search runs on its summary terms, sessions_fts returns the row via the unchanged replaceFtsRow path
Archived-row jsonlPath: construct the Claude fallback path only for claude-code rows

expected_outputs: typecheck completes without errors
route tests pass including the new archived-ohmypi jsonlPath assertions

narrative.Action: Implement PRD WI-6 step 1, per D-8. In mapArchivedConversation (src/dashboard/server/routes/conversations.ts:2371) change `jsonlPath: row.discoveredJsonlPath ?? (row.claudeSessionId ? sessionFilePath(row.cwd, row.claudeSessionId) : null)` to guard the fabricated fallback on harness: `jsonlPath: row.discoveredJsonlPath ?? (row.harness === 'claude-code' && row.claudeSessionId ? sessionFilePath(row.cwd, row.claudeSessionId) : null)`. listArchivedConversationsWithEnrichment (src/lib/overdeck/conversations.ts:1039) already joins on locator; verify it selects c.harness and thread it through the row type if not yet exposed. This bead is independent of the discovery pipeline and may land first. Tests: an archived ohmypi row maps jsonlPath=null pre-scan and the real discovered path post-scan (never a ~/.claude/projects path); an archived claude-code row keeps today's fallback behavior.

items.title: Given an archived ohmypi/codex conversation with no discovered row, when the archived list is fetched, jsonlPath returns null (never a fabricated ~/.claude/projects path)
Given an archived non-Claude conversation whose session was discovered, when the archived list is fetched, jsonlPath returns the real discovered path
Given an archived claude-code conversation, jsonlPath preserves today's sessionFilePath fallback behavior
resolveSessionFile: discovered-sessions fallback by locator after harness-specific resolution fails

expected_outputs: typecheck completes without errors
route tests pass including the agent-dir-removed pi transcript fallback case

narrative.Action: Implement PRD WI-6 step 2, per D-9. In resolveSessionFile (src/dashboard/server/routes/conversations.ts:749), after the existing harness-specific branches (ohmypi → resolvePiSessionPath, codex → resolveCodexRolloutPath, Claude launcher-pinned session id; lines 757-786) all fail, add a final fallback: look up discovered_sessions.jsonl_path by the conversation's locator (conversation_files.locator) through the existing data layer (add a small read helper to src/lib/overdeck/discovered-sessions.ts if one does not exist — one read via the existing door, no new direct SQLite access), and verify the file still exists (fs/promises access) before returning it. The messages endpoint's harness-dispatched parsing (getCachedMessages, conversations.ts:450) is unchanged — rendering already works; only resolution gains the fallback. Test: the messages endpoint serves a pi transcript whose agent dir was removed but whose file was discovered into discovered_sessions.

items.title: Given a managed pi conversation whose agent dir was removed but whose session file exists and was discovered, when GET /api/conversations/:name/messages is called, it returns the parsed transcript via the discovered-sessions fallback
Given the discovered path no longer exists on disk, the fallback rejects it and resolution returns the same not-found result as today
Given a conversation resolvable by the existing harness-specific branches, resolution returns the same path as before (fallback only fires on failure)
Harness facet chips + table badge on the Sessions page (server-side filter through REST/RPC)

verify_commands: cd src/dashboard/frontend && npx vitest run src/components/conversations --silent
expected_outputs: typecheck completes without errors
RTL tests pass: harness facet chips render with counts and the badge shows for non-claude rows

narrative.Action: Implement PRD WI-7 on today's surface (D-11 resolved: sessions-feed.ts has NOT landed). Backend: add harness to ConversationFilter in contracts (packages/contracts/src/types.ts) and to buildFilterSql in src/lib/overdeck/discovered-sessions.ts (WHERE harness = ? when set); accept the param in routes/discovered-sessions.ts; include harness counts in whatever facet-count query feeds FacetPanel. Rebuild contracts after editing. Frontend: add a 'Harness' facet group to src/dashboard/frontend/src/components/conversations/FacetPanel.tsx (chips with counts, matching existing facet-group markup); add a harness badge column to SessionTable.tsx for non-claude rows — squared badge per the pan-style-guide skill (no pill radius, no decorative color; monospace label like 'codex'/'ohmypi'). Filtering must be server-side (the param narrows the SQL, not a client-side filter). RTL tests for both components.

items.title: Given rows across multiple harnesses, when a harness facet chip is selected, the list request carries the harness param and the server returns only matching rows (SQL-narrowed, not client-filtered)
FacetPanel renders a Harness group with per-harness counts when non-claude rows exist
SessionTable renders a squared harness badge for non-claude rows, styled per the dashboard style guide
Re-measure scan time with all harness roots; verify Claude path within noise of baseline

expected_outputs: PRD draft's '## Scan timing' section contains both baseline and post-change timings with a stated within-noise verdict for the Claude-only path
metadata.acJustification: Measurement-only closing bead; two criteria fully cover it.

narrative.Action: After the discovery/parser/correlation beads land: re-run the same system-scan timing procedure as the scan-baseline bead (same trigger, run twice) with all harness roots present. Record post-change timings under the existing '## Scan timing' section in .pan/drafts/pan-2224.md and post them as a comment on GitHub issue 2224 alongside the baseline. The Claude-only portion must be within noise of the baseline (compare a run with only the Claude root populated if total time is dominated by new roots — new roots adding time is expected and acceptable; only the Claude path must not regress). State the verdict explicitly.

items.title: The '## Scan timing' section in .pan/drafts/pan-2224.md and a comment on GitHub issue 2224 each contains the post-change timings alongside the baseline numbers
The comparison contains an explicit verdict that Claude-only scan time is within noise of the baseline, with the measurement method described
